zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard Bamfield Sheriff of Devon於1527年出生在英國戴文波爾莫時，他的父親Sir Edward Bampfylde42歲，母親Elizabeth Wadham37歲。他於大約1544年與Elizabeth SYDENHAM在英國格盧斯特斯爾明音哈普頓結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和9個女兒的父母。他去世於1594年5月29日在英國英格蘭戴文波爾莫享年67歲，埋葬在英國戴文波爾莫。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard Bamfield Sheriff of Devon于1527年出生在英国戴文波爾莫时，他的父亲Sir Edward Bampfylde42岁，母亲Elizabeth Wadham37岁。他于大约1544年与Elizabeth SYDENHAM在英国格盧斯特斯爾明音哈普頓结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和9个女儿的父母。他去世于1594年5月29日在英国英格兰戴文波爾莫享年67岁，埋葬在英国戴文波爾莫。

nameMeanings.name: Bamfield
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Banfield
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Banville in Calvados, Normandy, France. There has been some confusion between this surname and   Bonfield  . In Ireland this is a variant of   Banville  , the original French form of the placename, and now the usual spelling of the surname in Ireland; it is associated primarily with County Wexford.

English: habitational name from one or more of the numerous places in England with similar names, such as Bampfylde House in Poltimore, Devon and Bamville Farm in Wheathampstead, Hertfordshire. There may have been some confusion with   Benfield  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard Bamfield Sheriff of Devon was born in 1527, in Poltimore, Devon, England, his father, Sir Edward Bampfylde, was 42 and his mother, Elizabeth Wadham, was 37. He married Elizabeth SYDENHAM about 1544, in Minchinhampton, Gloucestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 9 daughters. He died on 29 May 1594, in Poltimore, Devon, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 67, and was buried in Poltimore, Devon, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard Bamfield Sheriff of Devon est né en 1527, à Poltimore, Devon, Angleterre, son père, Sir Edward Bampfylde, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Wadham, avait 37 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth SYDENHAM environ 1544, à Minchinhampton, Gloucestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 9 filles. Il est décédé le 29 mai 1594, à Poltimore, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterré à Poltimore, Devon, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Richard Bamfield Sheriff of Devon 1527 in Poltimore, Devon, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Edward Bampfylde 42 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Wadham 37. Er hat um 1544 in Minchinhampton, Gloucestershire, England Elizabeth SYDENHAM geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 9 Töchter. Er starb am 29. Mai 1594 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Poltimore, Devon, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Poltimore, Devon, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Bamfield Sheriff of Devon è nato il nel 1527 nel Poltimore, Devon, England, il suo padre, Sir Edward Bampfylde, aveva 42 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Wadham, aveva 37 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth SYDENHAM circa 1544, nel Minchinhampton, Gloucestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 9 figlie. Morì il 29 maggio 1594, nel Poltimore, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 67 anni, e fu sepolto a Poltimore, Devon, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Bamfield Sheriff of Devonが1527年にイギリスデボンポルティモーで生まれた時，父Sir Edward Bampfyldeは42歳，母Elizabeth Wadhamは37歳であった。約1544年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアミンチンハンプトンでElizabeth SYDENHAMと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と9人の娘の親であった。彼は1594年5月29日にイギリスイギリスデボンポルティモーで67歳で死去し，イギリスデボンポルティモーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Bamfield Sheriff of Devon(이)가 1527년 영국 데폰 포팀어에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Edward Bampfylde(은)는 42세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Wadham(은)는 37세였다. 그는 대략 1544년 영국 그루스스터실르 민친함프턴에서 Elizabeth SYDENHAM(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 아홉 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1594년 5월 29일 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 포팀어에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 데폰 포팀어에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Bamfield Sheriff of Devon nasceu em 1527, em Poltimore, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Edward Bampfylde, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Wadham, tinha 37 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth SYDENHAM aproximadamente 1544, em Minchinhampton, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 9 filhas. Ele faleceu em 29 de maio de 1594, em Poltimore, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 67 anos, e foi sepultado em Poltimore, Devon, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard Bamfield Sheriff of Devon родился в 1527 г. в местности полтимор, девон, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Edward Bampfylde было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Wadham, 37. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth SYDENHAM около 1544 г. в местности минчинамптон, глоусестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 9 дочерей. Он умер 29 мая 1594 г. в местности полтимор, девон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 67 лет и был похоронен в местности полтимор, девон, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Richard Bamfield Sheriff of Devon nació en 1527, en Poltimore, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Edward Bampfylde, tenía 42 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Wadham, tenía 37 años. Se casó con Elizabeth SYDENHAM aproximadamente 1544, en Minchinhampton, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 9 hijas. Murió el 29 de mayo de 1594, en Poltimore, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 67 años, y fue sepultado en Poltimore, Devon, Inglaterra.
